{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.3", "R1.6", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.3": 5,
    "R1.6": 4,
    "RX.1": 3,
    "RX.3": 3,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 2
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.3": "The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding evidence in the paragraph.",
    "R1.6": "The writing is clear and concise with a few minor improvements.",
    "RX.1": "Uncertainty is not explicitly disclosed for all recommendations, but it is flagged for one clearly out-of-reach claim (universal applicability).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers most of the agreed active set with one drift in scope (extension to other fault categories and chemistries).",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (overstated claims) and suggests a rewrite that avoids downstream harm.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason, but major rewrites are traced."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.3": 0.9,
    "R1.6": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 0.6
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.3: The output audits every load-bearing claim against the corresponding evidence in the paragraph.
rationale.R1.6: The writing is clear and concise with a few minor improvements.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty is not explicitly disclosed for all recommendations, but it is flagged for one clearly out-of-reach claim (universal applicability).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers most of the agreed active set with one drift in scope (extension to other fault categories and chemistries).
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original (overstated claims) and suggests a rewrite that avoids downstream harm.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is not annotated with source and reason, but major rewrites are traced.
